# Afterlife

> Credentials that outlive their owners.

**Afterlife** is a ghost-access auditor. It surfaces cloud, code, and identity credentials that should have been revoked but weren't — every API key generated by a contractor who left two years ago, every OAuth grant tied to a deprovisioned employee, every long-lived AWS access key on an account marked "suspended" in the IdP.

That class of forgotten access drives a disproportionate share of modern breaches: Uber 2022, Okta 2023, MOVEit, Snowflake 2024. Most tools in this space treat each system in isolation. Afterlife builds a unified identity graph across AWS, GitHub, and your IdP, then runs detection rules over the graph and ranks findings by blast radius.

## Status

`v0.1` — active development. Targets AWS + GitHub + Google Workspace.

## What it detects

| Rule | Severity | What |
|------|----------|------|
| `OFFBOARDED-OWNER` | Critical | Credential is active, but its owner is deprovisioned in the IdP |
| `UNUSED-CREDENTIAL` | High | Credential hasn't been used in N days |
| `NEVER-USED` | Medium | Credential has no last-used timestamp — created and forgotten |
| `UNROTATED-KEY` | Medium | AWS access key not rotated in >180 days |
| `STALE-OAUTH` | High | OAuth app hasn't made an API call in N days, scopes still granted |
| `PRIVILEGE-DRIFT` | Medium | IAM role permissions far broader than its 90-day usage profile |
| `ORPHANED-GITHUB` | High | GitHub PAT or deploy key belongs to a user no longer in the org |

Each rule's logic, false-positive notes, and remediation are in [docs/DETECTIONS.md](docs/DETECTIONS.md).

## Quickstart

> The CLI surface below is the target interface for v0.1. Collectors are stubbed pending Week 1 implementation.

```bash
pip install -e ".[dev]"
afterlife init
afterlife scan aws --profile my-profile
afterlife scan github --org my-org
afterlife scan idp --provider google
afterlife analyze
afterlife report --format json
```

## Architecture

```
  collectors/  ─►  SQLite  ─►  identity graph  ─►  rules engine  ─►  findings  ─►  report
     AWS                        (NetworkX)         (pluggable)
     GitHub
     Okta / GWS
```

See [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md).

## Why "Afterlife"

The credentials this tool finds shouldn't still be alive.
